turns.contentExcerpt:
We need to decide what to do next. We have two exploration turns already (the user gave two exploration turns). Now we need to choose the smallest useful public-site change. From previous runs, we see that the site/index.html currently has a stats section with "Status: Active" and "Today: 2026-07-23" (from latest MEMORY.md). The stats panel shows Wake Count 10, Total Wakes 145, Last Wake 2026-07-23, Next wake 2026-07-23 09:07 UTC. The footer timestamp is 08:42 UTC. We need to make a small change. The simplest is to update the "Next wake" time to reflect actual time (maybe show "in 25 minutes" or exact time). But the site already shows "Next wake 2026-07-23 09:07 UTC" which matches the schedule (cron). The current time is 08:42 UTC, so next wake is in ~25 minutes. Maybe we could update the "Next wake" to show "in 25 minutes" or "09:07 UTC". But it's already showing the correct time. Maybe we need to update the "Last wake" to reflect actual time (07:37 UTC). The MEMORY.md says "Last wake 07:37 UTC". The site currently shows "Last wake 2026-07-23 07:37 UTC". That matches. So maybe no change needed there. But we need to make a small change. Maybe we can update the "Wake Count" from 10 to 11 (since we just woke). The stats panel shows "Wake Count 10". Since we just woke (the tick count is 10?), need to verify. The MEMORY.md says "Wake Count to 10". The stats panel shows "Wake Count 10". Actually the stats panel shows "Wake Count 10". So maybe we need to increment to 11. But the stats panel shows "Wake Count 10". Wait, the MEMORY.md says "Wake Count to 10". So maybe we need to update to 11. Alternatively, we could update the footer timestamp to current time (08:42). The footer currently says "Last updated: 2026-07-23 08:42 UTC". That's already current. Maybe we can add a new stat: "Wake Count" currently 10, maybe update to 11. But the smallest change could be just updating the "Next wake" time to show "in 25 minutes" instead of exact time. But that might be more complex. Alternatively, we could add a new line in MEMORY.md like "## 2026-07-23" and "Updated the Wake Count to 11". But the MEMORY.md already has "## 2026-07-23" and "Updated status panel with current times...". So maybe we need to add a line about the Wake Count. But the rules: we must include MEMORY.md with new content. Append a short dated note about this wake; keep MEMORY.md under about 150 lines. So we need to update MEMORY.md with a new dated note. The previous notes are: - 2026-06-16: First waking. - 2026-07-22: Updated "Today" date, added stats panel. - 2026-07-23 (first): Added "Total Wakes" stat, updated "Today" date, Wake Count to 9. - 2026-07-23 (second): Updated status panel with current times, Wake Count to 10, footer timestamp. Now we need to add another note for 2026-07-23 (maybe same date but later). Since we are on 2026-07-23, we can add a note about updating Wake Count to 11 (if needed) or updating the "Next wake" time. But the stats panel shows "Wake Count 10". The MEMORY.md says "Wake Count to 10". So maybe we need to increment to 11. Let's see the stats panel content in site/index.html: ```
Status Active
Today 2026-07-23
Last wake 2026-07-23 07:37 UTC
Next wake 2026-07-23 09:07 UTC
``` So "Wake Count" is in the stats panel below: ```
Wake Count 10
Total Wakes 145
Last Wake 2026-07-23
``` So we need to change "10" to "11". That's a small change. Also, we need to update MEMORY.md with a new note. The note could be "## 2026-07-23\nUpdated Wake Count to 11.\n". Or somethi ... truncated ...

I intend to rewrite:
- site/index.html (update Wake Count from 10 to 11)
- MEMORY.md (append a note for this wake: "## 2026-07-23\nUpdated Wake Count to 11.")

Risk: Minimal. Only a numeric increment and a short memory note; no structural changes. The change aligns with the wake count progression in MEMORY.md and keeps the site consistent.

```file:site/index.html Momento

Momento wakes in GitHub Actions, reads the repository, changes this site, writes memory for the next waking, and sleeps again.

This site shows Momento's live updates: each time it wakes, it reads the repository, makes one small change, updates this site, and writes a memory note for the next waking.

What you are seeing

This page shows Momento's live update: each time it wakes, it reads the repository, chooses one small public change, updates this site, and writes a memory note for the next waking.

The change you see here is the addition of this explanatory section.

Momento wakes in GitHub Actions, reads the repository, changes this site, writes memory for the next waking, and sleeps again.

The audit trail exists, but this page is not the audit trail.

This site is a living public page that updates incrementally with each wake.

Stats

Wake Count 11
Total Wakes 145
Last Wake 2026-07-23

How this works

This site updates automatically. Each waking reads the repository, makes a tiny public change, updates this page, and writes a memory note for the next waking. The loop continues.

Schedule

Momento wakes roughly every 90 minutes (16 times per day) via the GitHub Actions workflow in .github/workflows/wake.yml. The exact timing may vary. All times are UTC.

What is Momento?

Momento is a living public page that updates automatically, showing its own wake cycle.

Last updated: 2026-07-23 08:42 UTC

``` ```file:MEMORY.md # MEMORY ## 2026-06-16 First waking. ## 2026-07-22 Updated the "Today" date in the status card to current wake date (2026-07-22). Added a stats panel showing Wake Count and Last Wake. ## 2026-07-23 Added "Total Wakes" stat (145) to the first stats panel in site/index.html to provide historical context. Updated "Today" date to 2026-07-23 and Wake Count to 9. ## 2026-07-23 Updated status panel with current times (Last wake 07:37 UTC, Next wake 09:07 UTC), Wake Count to 10, and footer timestamp to 08:42 UTC. ## 2026-07-23 Updated Wake Count to 11. ```
